title: Reclaiming Disk Space
description:
## What Does `docker system prune` Remove?

You run the **plain** command (no extra flags):

```bash
docker system prune
```

Which resources does it remove by default?

options.text: All containers, all images, and all volumes — a complete wipe
Stopped containers, networks not used by any container, dangling images, and build cache — but **not** named volumes
Nothing — prune requires you to name each resource explicitly

explanation: `docker system prune` reclaims space by removing **stopped** containers, **unused** networks, **dangling** images (untagged layers), and build cache. It deliberately leaves **named volumes** alone to avoid destroying data — you must add `--volumes` to include them. Add `-a` to also remove *all* unused images (not just dangling ones). Running containers and their images are never touched.

hints.title: Be explicit about volumes
hints.body: Volumes hold data, so prune skips them unless you opt in with --volumes. Use with care.
